zh.Newport Pagnell, Milton Keynes Borough,: 英國巴金哈姆爾紐波特 帕格爾
zh.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate是John Smythe的兒子，於1541年出生在英國戴文艾可謝特。他於大約1557年與Anne Lynford在英國英格蘭素福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1593年12月25日之前在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德，埋葬在英國維斯特莫藍德。

zh-hans.Newport Pagnell, Milton Keynes Borough,: 英国巴金哈姆爾紐波特 帕格爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate是John Smythe的儿子，于1541年出生在英国戴文艾可謝特。他于大约1557年与Anne Lynford在英国英格兰素福爾可结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1593年12月25日之前在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德，埋葬在英国維斯特莫藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Highgate
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English heigh(e) ‘high’ + gat(e) ‘gateway’ (Old English hēah + geat). The name may be topographic, denoting a dweller ‘(by the) high gate’, or toponymic for someone from a place so called, such as Highgate (Middx). Compare Hyatt .

variants.name: Hyatt
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a dweller ‘(by the) high gate’, from Middle English heigh(e), high(e) + yat(e), gat(e) (Old English hēah + geat).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate was born in 1541, in Exeter, Devon, England as the son of John Smythe. He married Anne Lynford about 1557, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died before 25 December 1593, in Westmorland, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate est né en 1541, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre, fils de John Smythe. Il a épousé Anne Lynford environ 1557, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 25 décembre 1593, à Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Westmorland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate wurde 1541 in Exeter, Devon, England, als Sohn von John Smythe geboren. Er hat um 1557 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Anne Lynford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 25. Dezember 1593 in Westmorland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Westmorland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate è nato il nel 1541 nel Exeter, Devon, England come figlio di John Smythe. Lui sposato Anne Lynford circa 1557, nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì prima del 25 dicembre 1593, nel Westmorland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Westmorland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgateは1541年にイギリスデボンエクセターでJohn Smytheの息子として生まれた。約1557年にイギリスイギリススフォルクでAnne Lynfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1593年12月25日以前にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドで死去し，イギリスウエストモーランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate(은)는 1541년 영국 데폰 세터에서 John Smythe의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1557년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 Anne Lynford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1593년 12월 25일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드에서 사망했으며 영국 웨스트모를랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate nasceu em 1541, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra filho de John Smythe. Ele casou-se com Anne Lynford aproximadamente 1557, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu antes 25 de dezembro de 1593, em Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Westmorland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate родился в 1541 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. Его отца звали John Smythe. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Lynford около 1557 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер перед 25 декабрь 1593 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности вестморланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Smythe Of Highgate nació en 1541, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra como hijo de John Smythe. Se casó con Anne Lynford aproximadamente 1557, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió antes de 25 de diciembre de 1593, en Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Westmorland, Inglaterra.
